source_leaf_rgb_evidence.selection: mip-0 atlas pixels with alpha greater than 0.5
source_leaf_rgb_evidence.interpretation: The project-owned leaf atlas contains readable olive-green source color; near-black corridor crowns are not explained by source RGB.

accepted_findings: The live Unreal texture reports alpha present, CompressionNoAlpha false, Sharpen5 mips, alpha-coverage scaling at 0.5, NeverStream true, and virtual texturing false.
A 4x opacity scale restores the retained near, mid, and runtime-far crown silhouettes under the same masked TwoSidedFoliage shader.
The 4x-alpha and constant-opacity captures differ in fewer than 0.05 percent of pixels, proving that 4x scaling effectively saturates the sampled corridor card opacity.
LeafOpacityScale and LeafOpacityOverride remain exposed with neutral defaults of 1 and 0 for controlled diagnostics and later bounded tuning.
rejection_reasons: Both restored silhouettes render as nearly black crown masses, increasing dark-image coverage to 1.12 percent in guide-seat and 2.83 percent in river-eye.
The 4x scale destroys useful alpha discrimination at corridor distance and is visually equivalent to the proof-only constant-opacity mode.
Neither mode produces leaf-shaped green breakup, plausible transmission, readable crown interiors, or photoreal distance response.
The material response still requires separate AO, normal, transmission, and lighting diagnostics before any corridor promotion.
next_iteration_requirements: Hold 4x alpha and leaf shadows off while testing AO influence, flat versus sampled two-sided normals, and a bounded diagnostic emissive independently.
Use the split to choose a physically lit material correction; diagnostic emissive may localize the issue but cannot be promoted as a substitute for correct lighting.
After fixing foliage response, lower opacity amplification until leaf-shaped breakup survives distance without converging to constant-opacity crown masses.
Repeat both corridor views before mixed-species ecology or production substitution.
